speaker: GARTER
text: Heaven, from thy endless goodness, send

speaker: GARTER
text: prosperous life, long, and ever happy, to the high

speaker: GARTER
text: and mighty princess of England, Elizabeth.

speaker: CRANMER
text: And to your royal Grace and the good queen,

speaker: CRANMER
text: My noble partners and myself thus pray

speaker: CRANMER
text: All comfort, joy, in this most gracious lady

speaker: CRANMER
text: Heaven ever laid up to make parents happy

speaker: CRANMER
text: May hourly fall upon you!

speaker: KING
text: Thank you, good lord Archbishop.

speaker: KING
text: What is her name?

speaker: CRANMER
text: Elizabeth.

speaker: KING
text: Stand up, lord.

speaker: KING
text: With this kiss take my blessing.

speaker: KING
text: God protect thee,

speaker: KING
text: Into whose hand I give thy life.

speaker: CRANMER
text: Amen.

speaker: KING
text: My noble gossips, you’ve been too prodigal.

speaker: KING
text: I thank you heartily; so shall this lady

speaker: KING
text: When she has so much English.

speaker: CRANMER
text: Let me speak, sir,

speaker: CRANMER
text: For heaven now bids me; and the words I utter

speaker: CRANMER
text: Let none think flattery, for they’ll find ’em truth.

speaker: CRANMER
text: This royal infant—heaven still move about her!—

speaker: CRANMER
text: Though in her cradle, yet now promises

speaker: CRANMER
text: Upon this land a thousand thousand blessings,

speaker: CRANMER
text: Which time shall bring to ripeness. She shall be—

speaker: CRANMER
text: But few now living can behold that goodness—

speaker: CRANMER
text: A pattern to all princes living with her

speaker: CRANMER
text: And all that shall succeed. Saba was never

speaker: CRANMER
text: More covetous of wisdom and fair virtue

speaker: CRANMER
text: Than this pure soul shall be. All princely graces

speaker: CRANMER
text: That mold up such a mighty piece as this is,

speaker: CRANMER
text: With all the virtues that attend the good,

speaker: CRANMER
text: Shall still be doubled on her. Truth shall nurse her;

speaker: CRANMER
text: Holy and heavenly thoughts still counsel her.

speaker: CRANMER
text: She shall be loved and feared. Her own shall bless her;

speaker: CRANMER
text: Her foes shake like a field of beaten corn

speaker: CRANMER
text: And hang their heads with sorrow. Good grows with her.

speaker: CRANMER
text: In her days every man shall eat in safety

speaker: CRANMER
text: Under his own vine what he plants and sing

speaker: CRANMER
text: The merry songs of peace to all his neighbors.

speaker: CRANMER
text: God shall be truly known, and those about her

speaker: CRANMER
text: From her shall read the perfect ways of honor

speaker: CRANMER
text: And by those claim their greatness, not by blood.

speaker: CRANMER
text: Nor shall this peace sleep with her; but, as when

speaker: CRANMER
text: The bird of wonder dies, the maiden phoenix,

speaker: CRANMER
text: Her ashes new create another heir

speaker: CRANMER
text: As great in admiration as herself,

speaker: CRANMER
text: So shall she leave her blessedness to one,

speaker: CRANMER
text: When heaven shall call her from this cloud of darkness,

speaker: CRANMER
text: Who from the sacred ashes of her honor

speaker: CRANMER
text: Shall starlike rise as great in fame as she was

speaker: CRANMER
text: And so stand fixed. Peace, plenty, love, truth, terror,

speaker: CRANMER
text: That were the servants to this chosen infant,

speaker: CRANMER
text: Shall then be his, and like a vine grow to him.

speaker: CRANMER
text: Wherever the bright sun of heaven shall shine,

speaker: CRANMER
text: His honor and the greatness of his name

speaker: CRANMER
text: Shall be, and make new nations. He shall flourish,

speaker: CRANMER
text: And like a mountain cedar reach his branches

speaker: CRANMER
text: To all the plains about him. Our children’s children

speaker: CRANMER
text: Shall see this and bless heaven.

speaker: KING
text: Thou speakest wonders.

speaker: CRANMER
text: She shall be to the happiness of England

speaker: CRANMER
text: An agèd princess; many days shall see her,

speaker: CRANMER
text: And yet no day without a deed to crown it.

speaker: CRANMER
text: Would I had known no more! But she must die,

speaker: CRANMER
text: She must, the saints must have her; yet a virgin,

speaker: CRANMER
text: A most unspotted lily, shall she pass

speaker: CRANMER
text: To th’ ground, and all the world shall mourn her.

speaker: KING
text: O lord Archbishop,

speaker: KING
text: Thou hast made me now a man. Never before

speaker: KING
text: This happy child did I get anything.

speaker: KING
text: This oracle of comfort has so pleased me

speaker: KING
text: That when I am in heaven I shall desire

speaker: KING
text: To see what this child does and praise my Maker.—

speaker: KING
text: I thank you all.—To you, my good lord mayor

speaker: KING
text: And you, good brethren, I am much beholding.

speaker: KING
text: I have received much honor by your presence,

speaker: KING
text: And you shall find me thankful. Lead the way, lords.

speaker: KING
text: You must all see the Queen, and she must thank you;

speaker: KING
text: She will be sick else. This day, no man think

speaker: KING
text: ’Has business at his house, for all shall stay.

speaker: KING
text: This little one shall make it holiday.
